entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume species being studied for drought tolerance and water use efficiency.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which water is lost from the plant to the atmosphere.

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The difference between the saturation vapor pressure and the actual vapor pressure of the air.

entities.name: EARLY PLANT VIGOR
entities.description: The growth and strength of the plant during its early stages of development.

entities.name: CROP WATER USE
entities.description: The total amount of water consumed by the crop during its growth cycle.

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT
entities.description: Drought stress occurring during the reproductive stage of the crop.

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The measure of the ease with which water moves through plant tissues.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil used in the experiments.

entities.name: HYDROPONIC CULTURE
entities.description: A method of growing plants in a nutrient-rich water solution without soil.

entities.name: ICC 4958
entities.description: A chickpea genotype characterized by early vigor and low transpiration rate.

entities.name: RIL 211
entities.description: A chickpea recombinant inbred line characterized by early vigor and low transpiration rate.

entities.name: ICC 1882
entities.description: A chickpea genotype characterized by late vigor and high transpiration rate.

entities.name: RIL 022
entities.description: A chickpea recombinant inbred line characterized by late vigor and high transpiration rate.

entities.name: WATER TRANSPORT PATHWAYS
entities.description: The biological routes, including apoplastic and symplastic, for water movement in plants.

entities.name: APOPLAST
entities.description: The space outside the plasma membrane through which water moves.

entities.name: SYMPLAST
entities.description: The inner cytoplasmic network through which water moves.

entities.name: AQUAPORINS
entities.description: Water channel proteins that facilitate water transport across membranes.

entities.name: DE-ROOTED SHOOT TREATMENT
entities.description: An experimental procedure where roots are removed to isolate shoot responses.

entities.name: AQUAPORIN INHIBITOR
entities.description: A chemical agent used to block the function of aquaporin proteins.

entities.name: ROOT HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of the roots to transport water from the soil to the shoot.

entities.name: EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
entities.description: The atmospheric demand for water, often linked to high VPD.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of selecting and crossing genotypes to achieve desired traits.
